ocr.text: In the evolving landscape of North American banking, the performance metrics reflected in the "Deposit Growth by Segment"
and "Cross-Border Transaction Volumes" data table provide crucial insights into market dynamics. The analysis reveals
significant growth trajectories across various segments, with Large Corporates exhibiting a noteworthy March 2025 growth rate
of 8.5%, while SMEs have demonstrated an even more impressive increase of 10.8% during the same period.
DepositGrowthOverview  BySegment
CustomerSegment  IndividualDeposits  CorporateDeposits
GrowthRate  March2025  PreviousYear  CurrentRatio  Debt/Equity  P/ERatio
RetailCustomers  15.2%  $2.5B  1.5  0.25  12.4
SMEs  [HIDDEN]  $1.8B  [HIDDEN]  0.30  10.9
LargeCorporates  [HIDDEN]  $4.2B  1.3  [HIDDEN]  14.2
TotalDeposits  11.5%  $8.5B  1.4  0.25  12.5
This robust growth is further underpinned by financial health indicators; for example, Large Corporates maintain a Debt/Equity
ratio of 0.20, reflecting prudent leverage levels. Additionally, the Current Ratio for SMEs stands at a healthy 1.8, indicating
strong liquidity management.
Cross-borderTransactions  Ratios&Indices  GrowthRate  VolumeAnalysis
TransactionCount  TotalValue  AverageSize  CurrencyFluctuation  USDExchangeRate  P/ERatio  CurrentRatio
The table also highlights cross-border transaction activities, with Q1 2025 recording a Total Value of USD 1,200 million,
showcasing the increasing importance of international transactions. Furthermore, fluctuations in currency values, such as the Q4
2025 Currency Fluctuation of $433.33K, and a USD Exchange Rate of 3.5%, underscore the complexities of operating in a
globalized economy. As these metrics illustrate, understanding the interplay between deposit growth and cross-border
transactions is essential for stakeholders aiming to navigate the competitive financial landscape effectively.
